raw_91_c3: A25 v2 dispatcher applied to 6-repo HXC artifact universe (anima 84 + nexus 106 + n6-architecture 61 + hexa-lang 41 + airgenome 40 + hive 51 = 383 files, 9,916,852 raw bytes). MEASURED aggregate 78.0496% byte-weighted, 379/379 byte-eq PASS (4 EMPTY skipped). Pre-A25-v2 anchor 76.07% (8f8197d5 witness) → MEASURED 78.05% = +1.98pp lift on full 6-repo deployment. Slice-only projection from 74dae9da witness was +0.45pp = 76.52%, full-deployment projection was +7.95pp = 84.02%. ACTUAL measured deployment delta is +1.98pp = 78.05% — between the two projections, closer to slice-only. raw 91 honest C3: the full-deployment projection of 84.02% (74dae9da event 8) was OVER-OPTIMISTIC because the byte-share decomposition assumed each repo's full corpus would route through A25 v2 with class distribution matching the original 4-class test slice. ACTUAL byte-share is dominated by json-heavy (5.26MB / 53%) which routes correctly to a18 with 91.31% saving (achieving 80% target on this class) and small-file (95 files / 20KB / 0.2%) which gets only 0.59% saving (try-revert protects against regression but A23 inner is barely breaking-even on this corpus). The 80% TARGET is NOT ACHIEVED MEASURED on byte-weighted full deployment; gap 1.95pp residual. Cumulative gap reduction from 30.90pp original anchor to 1.95pp current: 93.69% closure (up from 86.3% prior milestone).

raw_91_honest_c3_retraction: 74dae9da event 8 full-deployment projection of 84.02% was over-optimistic. The byte-share decomposition assumed slice-class lift rates would extend to full-corpus deployment. ACTUAL measured lift is +1.98pp not +7.95pp. Reasons: (1) text-heavy class on full corpus is 47.08% saving not 64% extrapolated (broader prose mix lowers compression ratio); (2) small-file class barely lifts (0.59% MEASURED vs 5.06% in slice — 80B header overhead dominates 200-500B files in production scope, even with try-revert); (3) mixed class (NEW, 3.1MB / 31% byte-share) was not in original projection table — it routes to a18 and gets 71.60% saving, contributing meaningfully but not at projected ceiling. The structural finding (per-class wrapper overhead reclaimed within 0.08-0.24pp of A18-direct on large classes) is PRESERVED, but the byte-weighted aggregate projection requires revision downward.
